Neon Nights evenings to bring together the political elite, Neon Nights modeling courses for young girls. Both were viable ideas, yet the latter spread its wings. Why? The answer is extremely prosaic. Neon Nights would have proved too risky even for the president. Once in a while an event like this can hit big, but in the long run he needed to wash the money generated by Neon Nights swimming in filth through some front organization and put it from one pocket to another. How did he do all this?

At the beginning of the investigation, we ran into a very insignificant clue. To a utility bill amassed by Neon Nights. It seemed like a no-brainer, and at least as incomprehensible, but at the time we only knew about one Neon Nights, and that Neon Nights didn't seem like a money sink at all. We didn't understand it. Then, the more details came to light, and the more certain the existence of the Neon Nights formations became, the clearer the picture became. The utility bill debts were not brought together by the club full of prostitutes, but by the one above, whose network operated legally throughout the country. Of course, this is not because of his poor financial situation either, but consciously. But who wants to pile up debt on purpose? How high can a model trainer's monthly energy needs be? So much so that you don't run on it from the tens of thousands of tuition fees? What is certain is that the contract between the energy provider and the Neon Nights trainers established an unrealistically high late fee. But then why did Neon Nights get into these extra costs again and again over the years? Clearly, in order to be able to pay as much money as possible to the service provider, whose owners include Edgar Mortenstein. And where did all this money come from?

Since extra payments were made to the model trainers via unnamed sponsors, we had to ask the question, whose interest was it to support a business that uses the paid amount to pay utility bills? Apparently to someone who made a lot more money behind the scenes than he let on, the Neon Nights club. And the goal was to return the money to the person from whom it started. The Mortensteins used this method to launder the income from prostitution and human trafficking, and this inevitably connects the president to the prostitute network, the existence of which he denied all along.

But how did Mortenstein build this network in the first place? Since his most important businesses are to be found in the real estate market in addition to energy, it was obvious to start the investigation with the exact identification of the Neon Nights club. Its building was located at 12 Rue d'Ulysse in the city center. It is true that there is now a parking garage in its place, according to documents issued by the Land Registry, the previous building was demolished in 2016, the year when, based on testimony, Jessica Marsh agreed with the president on the termination of the club. To this day, the owner of the parking garage and the lot is one of Edgar Mortenstein's companies. Therefore, the fact that the building that housed Neon Nights was owned by the president is beyond dispute. But why did the president even need to operate an illegal nightclub?

It is not possible to give a clear answer to this question, even if it is perhaps what concerns the public the most. One thing is for sure. With this club, he opened up new opportunities for his other businesses, of course it would be a mistake to approach this issue purely from a business perspective. It is clear that the president did not engage in human trafficking solely to increase his wealth, but also for personal reasons. It is not known exactly what circle was the primary target audience of the Neon Nights club. But as we heard the testimonies, it could also serve to build business and political relationships.

The role of the president was definitely crucial in the operation of the club. The operational management was obviously entrusted to the actors of the previously outlined structure, so the gentlemen managed the day-to-day, while with their work they were responsible for the club's management, which, according to our current knowledge, meant Edgar Mortenstein and his narrowest circle of unknowns.

Meanwhile, Jake Cooper also grew up. He couldn't leave his vomited youth behind him, and his father didn't want to help him with that either. Despite the fact that he maintained contact with his son, they did not become a real family later, their relationship remained, let's say, business, even though Jake always wanted more.

Jake was young in the Liberty district, where he made many acquaintances. This district is famous for the fact that it is mainly inhabited by people who struggle in their everyday lives and live from today to tomorrow. They tend to take every opportunity to make the next day a little easier. The example of Mary Morales also shows this well. However, Cooper's task at that time was not recruiting girls, rather he was looking for young boys for the business, who could be exploited just as easily as the incarcerated girls. They were promised a well-paying job, easy money that any young person could get their hands on. What was their business? Almost nothing. To contact girls who could be arranged for Neon Nights as a liaison, and then to continue standing. How was this work worth it? Hardly. They obviously didn't have the opportunity to hold a more important position, and human trafficking broke even the ambitious teenage souls, so more and more boys were needed.

This is what Jake Cooper tried to take more seriously. He thought that by doing this he could prove his father's ability, who would let him get closer to him, but nothing really came easily in the Liberty district. The neighborhood was close-knit. Mike Alioglu, Jake Cooper's friend, quickly understood what was going on around him and, like a good brother, tried to explain to Jake that he had chosen the wrong direction for himself, which obviously damaged Jake Cooper's authority in the neighborhood. Fatma Alioglu also testified about this when she said that Mike wanted to talk his friend out of joining Neon Nights, although he couldn't have known that Jake was the one who actually wanted to recruit him. Mike's murder was not committed by the neon nightsos bad boys who appear in our imaginations, but by Jake Cooper, who was burdened by this act with such an incalculable weight that his personality was permanently split in two. Thus was born Jefferson, the naive, idealistic personality from whom all horrors remained hidden, and who met and then fell in love with Jessica Marsh, whom Jake Cooper, to Jefferson's greatest sorrow, recruited into Neon Nights together with Alice Bucks. This must have taken a toll on Jefferson, and after a sanatorium treatment, he came to the Morriset couple as a foster son, so that he could finally have the family he had never been given before. That put Jake Cooper in the parking lot for a while.

We could get an insight into what happened in Neon Nights from the accounts of Mary Morales and other girls. Exploitation, coercion, abuse. It is unnecessary to list them again in more detail, because the testimonies exactly support the charges that the prosecutor's office formulated against the president. Let's deal with the circumstances of Jessica Marsh's death, which can clearly be linked to the complaint against Edgar Mortenstein.

In October 2021, Jessica instructed her lawyer Bill Thomson that if she were to die unexpectedly, or based on what happened, if she were to be killed; immediately give a written statement to the head of the investigation against the President. But what could have happened in October to make Jessica Marsh feel the need to provide such security? Did he simply decide to report the president and expect to get in trouble for it? Or did Jake Cooper have something to do with it? After all, they had been together all along. And Cooper had to know what he was up to.

After he made the complaint, he was threatened several times. Once in Love City and once in the Montaukville house under police surveillance. In the first case, anyone could have dropped the threatening message in the mailbox, but not in Motaukville. There, only Jake Cooper and Jessica Marsh had the opportunity to do so without being noticed. But which of them could? Well, according to the handwriting expert, the messages were written by Jessica herself. Maybe he wanted to mislead the police?

We must not forget that Jessica Marsh was a smart woman. He knew exactly what was going on around him and how to get us information. Jessica wanted to let her know that Jake had threatened her. But because he wanted to keep Jake Cooper's identity a secret because of his emotions, he had no choice but to tell us.

Against Jake, Jessica could only trust Jefferson, whose idea that Jessica should withdraw the complaint turned out to be insufficient. Although Jessica knew exactly that she was still in great danger and that Jake wanted to kill her, she did not want to run away from him. He waited and hoped that Jefferson would intervene if there was a problem, but in the end he couldn't. On February 12, 2022, Jake Cooper killed Jessica Marsh.

And so we arrived at the overview of the circumstances of the car accident. According to Jefferson Morriset's testimony, he and Jessica were on their way to a show in Riverdale that night, when shortly after Montaukville, a black Ford Mustang began following them, then pushed their car off the road at the tunnel, and they crashed into the entrance of the tunnel. Jessica died in the collision, and Jefferson only regained consciousness after a quarter of an hour, when passers-by found them. The road between the two settlements leads through a mountainous forest, where there is no traffic in the evening. He was not an eyewitness to the incident. The two tickets found in Jessica's bag prove that they were going to a performance after all. However, the circumstances of the accident, as well as the reports of the passengers of the two other cars that passed by later, cast doubt on the authenticity of Jefferson's story about the black Mustang. The interviewed witnesses did not see any cars on the mentioned road section. Where did the Mustang go then? How did the accident happen?

To find out, we have to go back to the morning of the day Margaret Morriset woke her son up after hearing a loud crash followed by a car alarm. According to him, a sports car ran amok in the city early in the morning and went after something. How for what? A golden Renault with a dislodged bumper. We know this from the testimony of Jefferson, who had never seen that car in the city before that, nor had anyone else in the area, and no one had ever seen it since. Jefferson was the only one who saw her near their house that morning. Meanwhile, traffic surveillance cameras did indeed capture a black Mustang in Motaukville with damage to the right front. So the collision really happened. But if that was the case, why didn't the owner of the Renault report the accident? Who was he anyway? And whose was the testimony that Jefferson gave?

When we talk about that day, we have to ask whether we are listening to Jefferson Morris or Jake Cooper. Since the expert opinion supports Jefferson's claim that Jake Cooper, as a dominant personality, knows everything that he does as Jefferson, but in the opposite case, this is not the case, but Jake Cooper is able to influence Jefferson Morris's memories and even make him believe in events that did not actually happen, so everything that Jefferson Morris presents as a confession must be treated with reservations, and in fact, we can say that from a legal point of view, what Jefferson Morris claims should not even be taken into account.
What then can be the reality? What happened the day Jessica Marsh was killed?

When Margaret Morriset woke her son to look at the stolen car, it wasn't Jefferson who went out, but Jake, who really played the piano to wake up the neighbors and search for the unknown golden Renault, but not because the car was really there, just for show. The black Ford Mustang had indeed been there that morning in Montaukville, presumably with Ross Wilson at the wheel, and had hit something near the Morriset house, but that something was Jefferson's car. This is how he got the injury, which the forensic vehicle expert also presented as problematic evidence, because in the situation in which the accident allegedly happened, he could not have been on the side of the car. The black Mustang then left Montaukville and never returned. However, his mark remained on the side of Jefferson Morris's BMW, and we already know how it got there, Ross Wilson drove into it around six thirty in the morning. And in the evening, Jake Cooper and Jessica Marsh sat in the car, until the passengers of the car that reported the accident woke Jefferson up. So Jake Cooper deliberately crashed into the tunnel wall with the handbrake on so that Jessica Marsh would die on impact, and then all he had to do was hide Jefferson Morris behind his mask.

But why did Jake Cooper kill Jessica Marsh? Was he afraid that his confession might get him into trouble? Jessica had no intention of sending the one she loved to prison, her motivations could only be interpreted against the president. He only gave information about Jake Cooper as a witness, and that was just the name we needed to look for. So what could have been Jake Cooper's motive?

The answer to this question is quite pathetic. All the more so because, as during the entire investigation, it also became evident during the trial that Edgar Mortenstein's emotional attachment to Jake Cooper is even less than what could be thought based on the events described above. While Jefferson Morris was truly in love with Jessica, Jake Cooper's feelings always drew him to his father, he was loyal to him all his life because he trusted that he would one day acknowledge him.

Until yesterday, we didn't understand Cooper's motives. We knew he'd been acting as a self-appointed guardian of Neon Nights lately, but we didn't know why. However, the result of yesterday's test explained everything. He craves his father's attention now as much as he did when he was a child, and he doesn't get it just as much as he did then. When the discrediting campaign against me started after Jake's revelation, I was sure that the information was taken from Jake by the Mortenstein team, because during our conversation, Jake Cooper mistakenly identified me as Alice Bucks, whom he and Jessica Marsh organized into the network in 2011. This information could not have reached the president from anyone else, especially not in such a short time. Later, during the interrogation, Jake made himself a suspect again by referring to me by that name again, so everyone found out that he had something to do with Neon Nights, because he knew another girl from there, Alice, in addition to Jessica.

So Jake carried the president's fate on his heart. He tried to do everything in his power to hinder the work of the investigators, going so far as to send an assassin with Ross Wilson to clear the head of the investigation out of the way. When it became clear to him that the police were looking for Wilson, he killed him instead so that he would not be able to testify as part of a plea deal about the previous cases that connected him to Neon Nights.

However, to no avail. With each murder, the suspicion that Jessica Marsh's testimony was true and that the leader of the Neon Nights network was Edgar Mortenstein, the president of our country, only grew stronger.

And where are we now? We know that Jake Cooper killed Jessica Marsh and Ross Wilson, and that Edgar Mortenstein's businesses laundered money from the Neon Nights club, where young girls were held captive and forced into prostitution for years. According to the prosecutor's office, the leader of this was the president himself. I therefore ask the Honorable Court to find Jake Cooper and Edgar Mortenstein guilty of the previously stated charges.

The room was waiting in heightened excitement to see what would happen, even though nothing was needed yet. The closing speeches of the lawyers were over and the court only had to make its verdict by tomorrow, yet you could feel the tension of those sitting in the room, it almost burst out of them in the disturbed silence.
Mortenstein's lawyer began his speech with a cynical smile on his face.

"Dear court! We listened to a very effective speech, as if we had read it in a book. And there, perhaps, the guilty will fall into such an explanation. However, I would like to draw everyone's attention to a very important point: The prosecutor did not bring up any evidence that would prove without a doubt that the Neon Nights club belonged to Edgar Mortenstein. Where is the document that supports the president's involvement in all this? Where is the fact that disproves the possibility that some aggrieved girls ganged up against the president to discredit him because of a past grievance? Until such evidence is presented, I believe that Mr. Edgar Mortenstein, President, should be presumed innocent of all charges."

People started whispering. Several people agreed with the lawyer, but before the idea of ​​the president's innocence could take over the audience, a familiar-looking, kind, but somewhat gray figure got up from one of the chairs and stumbled towards the judge's pulpit with unsteady steps. It was Bill Thompson, Jessica's lawyer. Unexpectedly, he asked to be heard as a witness by the judge, who at first rejected him without thinking, saying that the trial had already taken place, but the old man did not give up, insisting that yesterday he did not yet have the information that made him dare to come here. The judge finally allowed him to present what he had brought.

"Dear court!" he started As was said earlier today, Jessica Marsh contacted me in October 2021 in the case, which the prosecutor had already said; made arrangements in the event of his death. Among the documents he left there at the time were several files gathered in a sealed folder. Jessica loved to play. With everyone on their own level. He was right about that. Even though she knew my limitations, for some reason she seemed to want me to be a part of the prosecutor's success. Because believe me, he will definitely win now. "he smiled," Unlocking the locked folder was a simple puzzle, but try as I might, I couldn't figure out how to solve it. I'm not good at puzzles. I almost gave up when it suddenly made sense yesterday, after listening to every speech at the trial.
"What was the puzzle?" the judge inquired.
"Mortenstein's son," Bill shot back extremely briefly. A foft laughter drifted through the room.
"That's it?"

"Well, as long as someone thinks this simple instruction is a puzzle, it's hard, isn't it? Especially when everyone knows that the president has no children. If, however, it turns out that there is, the folder will also open quickly."
"And what was in this folder?" the judge wondered.

"Perhaps it will surprise you, but it is exactly what the lawyer desired earlier. A contract that Jessica made with the president, in which he guarantees each girl a million dollars. And although I don't know criminal law, according to the requirements of the law of obligations, what I am holding in my hand is undoubtedly a valid contract."

Part of me, perhaps, expected the people in the courtroom to erupt in cheers when Mortenstein was convinced of his guilt. He succeeded, they think, and they pat me on the shoulder appreciatively, because I still managed to bring the country's first man to his knees. However, the reality was much more sobering. There was no joy, no storm of applause, everyone, in an instant, saw the president for who he really was and was horrified by him. Mortenstein stood at the head of a country for a decade and a half, believing himself to be different from the people he led. People felt cheated and betrayed, and like at the end of a relationship, they first started blaming themselves: is it our fault? What did we screw up? How could all this happen? There were signs. When Jessica or Alice disappeared, how many people searched for them? Has anyone been interested in the fact that they have not been found after weeks?

chapters.title: Chapter Forty
paragraphs: Bill Thompson visited me in my office the next day. His annoyingly slow knock now felt strangely soothing.
"Come in!" I said.
He was holding a briefcase in his hand, which he immediately placed on my desk.
"I brought you something else" he started. "Jessica, but you know..."
Suddenly, I didn't know what I could know, but from the envelope he took out, I quickly understood that it was another letter.
"Jessica did a good job of shredding these messages" I noted. "Things would have been easier if you had given everything up front."

"I think this is how he wanted to leave his mark on the world" he answered. "With a trial arranged by him, where he settles with the one who ruined them. Like the cat playing with the mouse. Mortenstein thought he would win until the last moment."
"But Jessica didn't see that anymore" I hesitated.

"Haven't you known your girlfriend in all these years, Amy? It wasn't important to him to see him defeat Mortenstein. He needed to go to prison according to his rules. And so it was. In fact, Jessica had already solved this case before she even started. But don't feel bad about it."

"Oh, I don't feel it at all" I answered. "I know exactly why he did it. All his life he felt guilty for getting himself into this. He lived to fix this, and when he did, he needed no more. Twenty-seven years were enough for him to achieve what he wanted in life."
"You can see that" Bill agreed. "although he could have taken it to much more."
"I think if we accepted his help, we should also accept the way he wanted to live."
"Either way, this letter is still yours" he handed over what he had brought. I received a simple pale pink envelope.
"Is that all, or could there be another gift from him?" I wondered as I opened the letter.
"Not from me, I think" he smiled. "but I understand that you still have files with which you can start new cases."
"True" I answered. "I just don't know yet if I want to."
"Well, we'll see. Quite a lot has happened in the last six months."
"Would you mind if I read it now?" I asked Bill Thompson.
"Come on, no way."
Dear Amy!

If you are reading this letter, you must have won the lawsuit. I doubt Bill would disobey my instructions regarding my messages, so congratulations! Thank you for doing this, I wouldn't have gone anywhere without you. You are my masterpiece, and in retrospect I apologize for this presumptuous statement, because you owe everything to yourself and to Anton, who went to hell after you to bring you home. You know, it's easier on my conscience if I can believe that I had something to do with it. You got into this situation because of me, and I hope you will be able to forgive me for the many wrongs I have done to you. I was young and in love, even if that can't be an excuse for anything.

I also sent a letter to Jefferson. Everything had to be revealed. Jake could no longer hide behind her back, they both had to face it. I don't know what's next for him, but I wanted him to have something to remember me by. Keep an eye on them, you better know what's up with them!

As for us, it's hard for me to say anything. You were my family. I loved you more than anything, even if we never see each other again. It was nice to meet you the other day. It was as if we had never left each other, but that's how family is, at least that's how I imagine it.

Before it's all over, I want to give you one last thing. Not just for you, but for Bill too. When we were little, we often dreamed that our parents would one day put us in our room and take us to their home, like in a fairy tale where everything ends well. Although I could have helped you find your family, I have not been able to track them down. But I found Bill. He's my father, Amy, but no matter how I tried to tell him, I just couldn't. Bill is a good man. He didn't know he had a child, don't be mad at him because of me. But it can serve you well. I hope you will know what to tell him about me.

In the attachment you will find a few more passwords for the files that I encrypted. Several members of the organization can be caught with the materials, but I leave it up to you what you do with them.
I don't want to finish the letter yet. As soon as I stop, I know it will be my last word to you, and I'm not ready for that, but I don't want to waste time just talking.
What could I do? We will meet in the afterlife someday. I'm trying to get out so we can get into the same room.
Thank you for everything Amy, my dear Alice, my sweet little sister. i love you
February 14, 2024

...so I was sitting at the counter of Toro Ardiente, Love City's best Spanish skybar, in the white dress with a low cut in the back that I had bought last week especially for Anton, and I was thinking; what we said to each other at the beginning of our relationship, and of course how much has happened since then. For example, the fact that Anton recently set me up and wouldn't let me drink the two martinis that maybe I shouldn't have asked for in a Spanish bar, but I didn't have time to dwell on that, because we had a reservation for the presidential suite at the Corinthia Royal, where after an excitingly hot limousine ride, Anton, like a real gentleman, because he was, escorted me up to the floor in a suit, and although he should have finished his novel before that, he offers to read our story to me, unfortunately he doesn't have time for that anymore...
"So here we are. You've been working on this for the past year, and there's only a few lines left, if I understand correctly."
"That's right. I would have liked a striking ending, but I didn't have time to come up with one before leaving."
"I have an idea for it" I hurried to calm Anton down. "I thought how about I write the last dialogue for you."
"Do you have an idea how the story could end?"
"More like the novel" I answered. "The story would only really begin now, what do you say? Of course, two errors would have to be corrected first."
"And which ones would be about?"
"The Martinique, Anton. How should I drink alcohol when I'm expecting a baby?"
